summary: The IDF detained an unarmed Hamas suspect at the Kerem Shalom crossing, emphasizing no infiltration into Israeli territory. Israeli forces engaged and neutralized multiple Hezbollah militants operating from civilian cover on the Ali Taher ridge. A night operation in Bethlehem seized over 250 weapons and 40 manufacturing kits.
key_points: The IDF detained an unarmed Hamas operative near the Kerem Shalom border crossing, stating there was no infiltration into Israeli territory (Source: @manniefabian/58668).
Israeli forces shot at four Hezbollah militants on the Ali Taher ridge after they crossed into the security zone, resulting in at least one casualty (Source: @New_security8200/202068).
A night operation in Bethlehem seized more than 250 weapons and 40 weapon‑manufacturing kits intended for use throughout Judea and Samaria (Source: @idf_telegram/24425).
Four Hamas‑linked rocket launch positions were destroyed in Gaza during the latest night of hostilities (Source: @pkpoi/142948).
Two Hezbollah gunmen were targeted and killed by an Israeli air strike after being spotted near Israeli troops in the Ali Taher area (Source: @manniefabian/4240).
Hamas is reportedly constructing additional rocket launch positions in Gaza in preparation for a new campaign against Israel (Source: @almogboker78/17579).
Hezbollah continues to operate from civilian cover in Lebanese territory, using civilian infrastructure to shield its activities (Source: @New_security8200/202068).

sections.executive_summary: The IDF detained an unarmed Hamas suspect at Kerem Shalom, confirming no breach of Israeli territory. Israeli forces engaged Hezbollah militants on the Ali Taher ridge, resulting in casualties and a confirmed hit. A night raid in Bethlehem seized over 250 weapons and manufacturing kits, indicating a significant buildup of illicit arms in the West Bank.

points: More than 3 million Iranian pilgrims visited Karbala for Ashura, with 400 000 crossing the Iran‑Iraq land border on a single day (Source: @naya_foriraq/79781).
Thousands of mourners participated in the Tasu‘a commemoration in Ardabil, Iran (Source: @presstv/195426).
Iranian authorities reported a sharp rise in bread prices in Tehran and Varamin, with loaves reaching up to 2 700 IRR (Source: @VahidOnline/76615).
Iranian officials discussed ongoing negotiations with Hamas leaders regarding Gaza, emphasizing the “continuous aggression” of occupiers (Source: @asafroz/50680).
The IDF detained an unarmed Hamas operative near the Kerem Shalom crossing, emphasizing that no Israeli territory was breached (Source: @manniefabian/58668).
Israeli troops shot at four Hezbollah militants on the Ali Taher ridge after they entered the security zone, with at least one fatality (Source: @New_security8200/202068).
A night operation in Bethlehem seized over 250 weapons and 40 weapon‑manufacturing kits intended for distribution across Judea and Samaria (Source: @idf_telegram/24425).
Four Hamas‑linked rocket launch sites were destroyed in Gaza during the latest night of fighting (Source: @pkpoi/142948).
Two Hezbollah gunmen were killed by an Israeli air strike after being identified near Israeli forces in the Ali Taher area (Source: @manniefabian/4240).
Israeli officials reiterated that Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon even if requested by the United States (Source: @yediotnews25/131867).
Hamas is reportedly constructing new rocket launch positions in Gaza in preparation for a forthcoming campaign against Israel (Source: @almogboker78/17579).
Four Hamas‑linked rocket launch positions were destroyed by Israeli strikes during the recent night of hostilities (Source: @pkpoi/142948).
Israeli forces seized more than 250 weapons and 40 weapon‑manufacturing kits in Bethlehem, indicating a significant illicit arms cache in the West Bank (Source: @idf_telegram/24425).
Israeli troops arrested 19 wanted individuals and confiscated over 250 weapons in the Etzion brigade area of Hebron earlier this year (Source: @idf_telegram/24425).
Hezbollah militants operating from civilian cover on the Ali Taher ridge were engaged by Israeli forces, resulting in at least one casualty (Source: @New_security8200/202068).
Two Hezbollah gunmen were targeted and killed by an Israeli air strike after being spotted near Israeli troops in the Ali Taher area (Source: @manniefabian/4240).
Lebanese sources reported a bomb explosion in the MAG field in the Naameh area (Source: @redlinkleb/28045).
Israeli Defense Minister stated Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon even if the United States requests it (Source: @yediotnews25/131867).
Iranian officials discussed ongoing diplomatic contacts with Hamas leadership regarding Gaza and highlighted continued aggression by occupiers (Source: @asafroz/50680).
Qatari Prime Minister is reported to be discussing a meeting in Muscat involving Iran, the Gulf Cooperation Council, and Iraq concerning the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @naya_foriraq/79775).
Iranian authorities noted that over 3 million pilgrims traveled to Karbala for Ashura, reflecting large cross‑border movement (Source: @naya_foriraq/79781).
Russian officials praised the Iran‑US agreement as constructive for the region (Source: @SharghDaily/167863).
U.S. Secretary of State discussed with UAE leadership a deal involving Iran, the Strait of Hormuz, and regional stability (Source: @N12chat/214422).
Trump announced that Iran has agreed not to impose tolls or charges on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4249).
Iranian officials reported that 400 000 Iranian pilgrims crossed the land border into Iraq on a single day (Source: @naya_foriraq/79781).
U.S. officials emphasized that no country may charge fees for passage through international waterways, referencing the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @mamlekate/103760).

key_developments: IDF detained an unarmed Hamas operative at Kerem Shalom, confirming no infiltration into Israeli territory (Source: @manniefabian/58668).
Israeli forces engaged and killed multiple Hezbollah militants on the Ali Taher ridge after they entered the security zone (Source: @New_security8200/202068).
A night raid in Bethlehem seized over 250 weapons and 40 manufacturing kits, indicating a large illicit arms cache in the West Bank (Source: @idf_telegram/24425).
Four Hamas‑linked rocket launch sites were destroyed in Gaza during the latest night of hostilities (Source: @pkpoi/142948).
Two Hezbollah gunmen were eliminated by an Israeli air strike after being identified near Israeli troops in the Ali Taher area (Source: @manniefabian/4240).
Hamas is reportedly constructing additional rocket launch positions in Gaza in preparation for a new campaign (Source: @almogboker78/17579).
Israel's Defense Minister reaffirmed that Israel will not withdraw from southern Lebanon even if requested by the United States (Source: @yediotnews25/131867).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to exploit civilian infrastructure in southern Lebanon to launch attacks, as demonstrated by multiple engagements on the Ali Taher ridge. Hamas is expanding its rocket launch capabilities in Gaza, suggesting an escalation in firepower for future operations. The large seizure of weapons in Bethlehem indicates a substantial illicit arms network in the West Bank that could fuel further militant activity.
sections.regional_response: Iran is maintaining high-profile religious pilgrimages and diplomatic outreach, signaling continued regional influence. Gulf states, including the UAE, are engaging with Iran and the United States over Strait of Hormuz security, while the U.S. emphasizes free navigation. Turkey's leadership publicly condemned Israeli actions, framing them as obstacles to regional peace.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Hezbollah movements in southern Lebanon should focus on civilian cover tactics and potential spillover into Israeli border zones. Hamas construction of new launch sites warrants increased surveillance of Gaza's northern sectors. The West Bank weapons cache suggests organized supply chains that may be linked to both local and external militant groups.
